title: Unstable Merging Sort with Incorrect Swap

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers `nums`, implement merging sort to sort it in non-decreasing order. The function `sortArray` should return a new sorted array. However, the implementation contains a logical error.
statement.inputFormat: An array of integers `nums` with at least one element.
statement.outputFormat: A new sorted array of integers.
statement.notes: The implementation should use the merging sort algorithm and return a new sorted array. The logical error is related to the way the merge process is implemented.

examples.explanation: The function sorts the input array in non-decreasing order.

hints.text: Verify the merge implementation to identify the logical error.

editorial.title: Merging Sort: Correct Implementation
editorial.intuition: The merging sort algorithm sorts the input array by dividing it into two halves and recursively sorting each half before merging them.
editorial.bruteForce: Iterate through the input array to find incorrect merges.
editorial.betterSolution: Verify the merge process to ensure correct data merging.
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement the merging sort algorithm correctly.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Verify that the merging sort implementation is correct.
editorial.dryRun: Example run through the merging sort algorithm.

starterCode.javascript: function mergeSort(arr) { // TODO: implement merging sort algorithm

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: vector<int> sortArray(vector<int>& nums) { if (nums.size() < 2) return nums; vector<int> left(nums.begin(), nums.begin() + nums.size() / 2), right(nums.begin() + nums.size() / 2, nums.end()); merge(left, right, nums.begin()); return nums; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution { public int[] sortArray(int[] nums) { if (nums.length < 2) return nums; int[] left = Arrays.copyOfRange(nums, 0, nums.length / 2), right = Arrays.copyOfRange(nums, nums.length / 2, nums.length); merge(left, right, nums); return nums; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def sortArray(self, nums: list[int]) -> list[int]: if len(nums) < 2: return nums left, right = nums[:len(nums) // 2], nums[len(nums) // 2:] merge(left, right, nums); return nums;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function mergeSort(arr) { if (arr.length < 2) return arr; let left = arr.slice(0, arr.length / 2), right = arr.slice(arr.length / 2); merge(left, right, arr); return arr; }
